title: The One Where They're Going to Party!

jokes.text: What do you want to show us? Because all I can see is this bitchen van!
jokes.setup: Character asked what they're supposed to see
jokes.punchline: They see exactly what they're supposed to see but phrase it as if they're missing something
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection structure - sets up expectation of confusion then reveals they see exactly what they should. 'Bitchen' adds character flavor.

jokes.text: We'll replace the sword with a baguette.
jokes.setup: Van apparently has a sword painted on it from previous owner
jokes.punchline: Casually planning to replace weapon with bread for catering business
jokes.explanation: Efficient visual comedy - implies the van has ridiculous fantasy artwork that needs minor modification for food service.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I think I lost my virginity in that van.
jokes.setup: Looking at what appears to be their new business van
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of intimate history with the vehicle
jokes.explanation: Classic oversharing moment. The casualness of the delivery against the intimate revelation creates the humor.

jokes.text: Oh yeah, I definitely know her.
jokes.setup: Previous line about losing virginity in the van
jokes.punchline: Different character confirms knowledge of the person who made the revelation
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure that extends the previous joke by implying shared knowledge/history.

jokes.text: Sure, Cilantro Larry!
jokes.setup: Reference to cooking school guy who put cilantro in everything
jokes.punchline: Nickname that reduces his identity to his one culinary quirk
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - instantly creates a memorable character through a descriptive nickname. Very quotable.

jokes.text: ...dozens of people!
jokes.setup: Character talking about amazing opportunity to influence...
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small audience reach for food critic position
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with deliberate pause. The deflation from 'amazing opportunity' to 'dozens' is well-crafted.

jokes.text: That was me, hopping on board.
jokes.setup: Character presumably makes some gesture of agreement
jokes.punchline: Explicitly narrating their own subtle action for comic effect
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about explaining one's own actions. Shows character self-awareness while being understated.

jokes.text: There's this thing about bees on the Discovery Channel.
jokes.setup: Ross asked if he's ready to party
jokes.punchline: His idea of exciting plans is a nature documentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy for Ross - his nerdy preferences revealed through what he considers party-worthy entertainment.

jokes.text: Gandalf? Gandalf is coming to town?
jokes.setup: Casual mention of someone named Gandalf
jokes.punchline: Extreme excitement over this person's arrival
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal conversation to extreme enthusiasm. Sets up the Gandalf running gag.

jokes.text: Wow, okay. Dude alert!
jokes.setup: Characters getting overly excited about partying
jokes.punchline: Someone calling out their sudden bro-ish behavior
jokes.explanation: Efficient observational humor about masculine bonding behavior. 'Dude alert' is a memorable phrase.

jokes.text: Mike 'Gandalf' Ganderson. Only the funnest guy in the world.
jokes.setup: Mystery about who Gandalf is
jokes.punchline: Mundane real name with grandiose description
jokes.explanation: Contrast between ordinary name and mythical nickname, plus the hyperbolic 'funnest guy in the world' description.

jokes.text: Remember those strippers? And you paid me $50 to eat that book?
jokes.setup: Character defending that they do have fun together
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre examples of their 'fun' activities
jokes.explanation: Escalation from semi-normal (strippers) to completely absurd (eating books for money). Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: No, I had sex in high school.
jokes.setup: Asked if they read Lord of the Rings in high school
jokes.punchline: Implies reading and sex were mutually exclusive activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and structure. The matter-of-fact delivery of the implication that nerdy interests and sex don't mix is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Neither can you, Sophie, but for different reasons.
jokes.setup: Someone saying they can't stay an assistant forever
jokes.punchline: Ominous implication about Sophie's job security with casual delivery
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace humor delivered with perfect casualness. The 'different reasons' makes it more ominous and funnier.

jokes.text: -Really? -No.
jokes.setup: Someone asking for confirmation about sleeping with someone for a job
jokes.punchline: Immediate, flat denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy. The blunt 'No' after building suspense is well-executed.

jokes.text: Yeah. I mean, no.
jokes.setup: Just denied sleeping with someone for a job
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts themselves then corrects again
jokes.explanation: Extends previous joke by showing character's confusion/indecision about their own story.

jokes.text: Kidding!
jokes.setup: Inappropriate suggestion about needing convincing for job
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was supposedly a joke
jokes.explanation: Weak structure - uses 'kidding' as escape hatch rather than earning the laugh. Creates cringe but not much humor.

jokes.text: You go out for beer and end up on a boat to Nova Scotia.
jokes.setup: Explaining how unpredictable Gandalf is
jokes.punchline: Extreme example of spontaneous adventure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane (beer) to absurd (Nova Scotia boat trip). Specific geographic detail adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I got my passport, fresh socks, and a snakebite kit.
jokes.setup: Character preparing for night out with Gandalf
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous preparation items
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from practical (passport) to mundane (socks) to survival gear (snakebite kit). Shows character's anxiety about Gandalf's unpredictability.

jokes.text: Somewhere maybe along the equator?
jokes.setup: Asked where they'll be around noon for meeting up
jokes.punchline: Suggests being anywhere on Earth's circumference as casual meeting spot
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd expectations about Gandalf's adventures. The casual tone contrasts well with the impossible logistics.

jokes.text: Chicago's so lucky.
jokes.setup: Gandalf can't come because he's stuck in Chicago
jokes.punchline: Treating Chicago as fortunate to have him trapped there
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing devotion to Gandalf. Efficient delivery with nice perspective flip.

jokes.text: Stupid, useless... Canadian money!
jokes.setup: Character expressing frustration
jokes.punchline: Blaming Canadian currency for unrelated problem
jokes.explanation: Misdirection works well - builds up anger then reveals absurd target. The specificity of Canadian money adds to the randomness.

jokes.text: It did brighten up the filing cabinets.
jokes.setup: Interviewer praising Rachel's filing system implementation
jokes.punchline: Rachel reducing her achievement to mere decoration
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's character - modest about her accomplishments to the point of self-deprecation. Works for character development.

jokes.text: She gets it right almost every time.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioned getting Joanna's bagels as responsibility
jokes.punchline: Damning with faint praise about basic task competency
jokes.explanation: Perfect passive-aggressive boss humor. 'Almost every time' suggests frequent failure at the simplest task.

jokes.text: Rachel can carry two things at once.
jokes.setup: Continuing to sabotage Rachel's interview
jokes.punchline: Treating basic motor skills as impressive accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Escalates the sabotage by praising the most basic human capability as if it's special. Very quotable workplace humor.

jokes.text: With them. Under them. What's the difference?
jokes.setup: Discussion about working with designers
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo disguised as work relationship clarification
jokes.explanation: Continues Joanna's sabotage with sexual suggestion. Somewhat predictable innuendo but fits character's malicious intent.

jokes.text: Would I go back to Allesandro's? Sure, I'd get two meals. One for me, and one for the guy pointing the gun to my head.
jokes.setup: Reading negative restaurant review
jokes.punchline: Escalates to extreme scenario requiring armed coercion to return
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with perfect setup/punchline. The specificity of 'two meals' makes the gunpoint scenario funnier.

jokes.text: Because it would be silly to take it out if we're just gonna put it back in again.
jokes.setup: Discussion about keeping a waterbed in the van
jokes.punchline: Innocent logic that sounds sexually suggestive
jokes.explanation: Relies on accidental innuendo rather than crafted humor. The double meaning is obvious but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I couldn't eat it. My five friends couldn't eat it, and one of them eats books.
jokes.setup: Defending harsh restaurant review
jokes.punchline: References earlier absurd detail about someone eating books
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that gives the earlier absurd detail new context. Good escalation showing how bad the food must be.

jokes.text: The waiter carried the breadsticks in his pants.
jokes.setup: Defending criticism of restaurant service
jokes.punchline: Specific gross hygiene violation
jokes.explanation: Specific disgusting detail that justifies the harsh review. Good visual comedy and very quotable.

jokes.text: You should serve it with vodka and celery.
jokes.setup: Criticizing marinara sauce as tasting like tomato juice
jokes.punchline: Suggests turning it into bloody mary cocktail instead
jokes.explanation: Clever culinary insult that works on multiple levels. Shows Monica's food knowledge while delivering a creative burn.

jokes.text: Congratulations. You crossed the line into completely useless. Get out.
jokes.setup: Sophie trying to be helpful in the confrontation
jokes.punchline: Brutal, matter-of-fact firing
jokes.explanation: Perfect workplace cruelty delivered with clinical precision. 'Completely useless' is harsh but the casual 'Get out' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: ...-ing binge.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking what was said about her drinking
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to cover escalating the accusation
jokes.explanation: Shows Joanna making things worse by trying to cover her lie. The awkward pause and addition makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I balanced my checkbook. And I gave first names to all the foosball players.
jokes.setup: Character asked if they've been sitting around all day
jokes.punchline: Increasingly pathetic domestic activities presented as accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from mundane (checkbook) to absurd (naming foosball players). Shows character's desperate attempts to feel productive.

jokes.text: Those were seals, man.
jokes.setup: Character remembering cold morning with dogs licking face
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'dogs' were actually seals
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds absurd detail to earlier Gandalf adventure reference. The correction is matter-of-fact and funny.

jokes.text: It would totally depend on her coloring and...
jokes.setup: Asked how suit would look on assistant buyer
jokes.punchline: Taking the question literally as fashion advice rather than celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - missing the subtext and focusing on practical fashion considerations. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: I'm going to have walls!
jokes.setup: Rachel excited about new office
jokes.punchline: Getting excited about most basic architectural feature
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's low expectations from current work situation. Getting excited about walls reveals how bad her current setup must be.

jokes.text: ...with empty trays, asking for money.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining what catering business would be without Monica
jokes.punchline: Reducing it to essentially begging with props
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical conclusion to the argument. Reduces catering to its absurd essence without the actual cooking component.

jokes.text: Thank God! I'm exhausted.
jokes.setup: Characters supposedly having fun partying
jokes.punchline: Relief at admitting they're tired from 'fun' activities
jokes.explanation: Good irony about party exhaustion. The relief in the voice makes the admission funnier.

jokes.text: I don't want to be up too late, so I'll have a decaf.
jokes.setup: Characters at coffee shop after 'partying'
jokes.punchline: Ordering decaf to avoid staying up late while supposedly partying
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony of ordering decaf during a night of 'wild partying'. Shows how tame they actually are.

jokes.text: I used them as mittens. I didn't want to touch a thing in that last place.
jokes.setup: Character explaining why they brought extra socks
jokes.punchline: Using socks as protective gear due to venue grossness
jokes.explanation: Pays off earlier setup about bringing extra socks. Shows their 'party venues' were actually disgusting.

jokes.text: And so what if I like to go home, put on Kenny G and take a bath?
jokes.setup: Character defending their lifestyle choices
jokes.punchline: Admitting to stereotypically uncool relaxation habits
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Kenny G specifically chosen as ultimate un-cool music choice. Relatable adult confession.

jokes.text: We're 29. We're not women.
jokes.setup: Characters defending their domestic preferences
jokes.punchline: Implying women would have different expectations at that age
jokes.explanation: Weak humor that relies on gender stereotypes without much wit. The line feels dated and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: And I really don't want to be the reason I'm unhappy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining she doesn't want to make Monica unhappy
jokes.punchline: Accidentally makes it about her own happiness through word confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - starts altruistic but accidentally makes it self-centered. The confusion feels genuine to her character.

jokes.text: Yes. So close!
jokes.setup: Mrs. Lynch commenting that Rachel seems close to deceased Joanna
jokes.punchline: Rachel enthusiastically agreeing despite their hostile relationship
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Rachel has to fake grief. The enthusiasm contrasts well with the actual relationship they had.

jokes.text: Nothing about an assistant buyer?
jokes.setup: Rachel supposedly grieving Joanna's death
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking about job opportunity despite the tragedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - Rachel's priorities showing through fake grief. The timing makes it appropriately uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Of course, that literally is the case for several of you.
jokes.setup: Allesandro saying firing chef is like losing family member
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging that restaurant staff are actually his relatives
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about family restaurant cliches. The matter-of-fact delivery makes the acknowledgment funnier.

jokes.text: Are you going to kill me?
jokes.setup: Monica's fancy speech about cooperation and recipes
jokes.punchline: Awkward pause followed by panicked question to hostile staff
jokes.explanation: Good cringe ending - Monica's prepared speech meets reality of angry staff. The direct question breaks tension comically.

tmdb_overview: Ross and Chandler's college friend cancels, so they throw their own party. Rachel misses a promotion. Monica becomes head chef or keeps her catering business.
